hostClass: GitHub-hosted macOS runner (Apple Silicon, ~3-core virtual), recorded from a Nightly run
note: Recorded on the GitHub-hosted runner the Nightly workflow actually uses, so the nightly smoke gates against numbers from its own host class rather than a developer machine. It gates on ONE metric, bulk-single throughput, which on that runner is stable (about 4 percent intra-run spread). The nanosecond lookup metrics (lookup-view, lookup-raw, session-lookup) are deliberately omitted: on a shared virtual host they swing 33 to 35 percent run to run, so gating on them would flap. The smoke still measures and prints them, and the comparator reports any metric absent from this file without gating. Tolerance is wide (35 percent) on purpose: this is a catastrophe canary for a real (roughly 2x or worse) decode regression, not a precise perf gate. Precise performance work runs the full iris-bench suite on a known host (methodology in Benchmarks/README.md). Comparison is one-sided: throughput fails only when median falls below baseline*(1-tolerance).
